transcript: [sample of Wayne Bent ] We are naked and unashamed.
Oh, boy! Yeah, there’s a lotta things happening tonight. Whole lotta things! Major Entertainer, what’s shakin’ up there? Can ya hear me okay?
Oh, we hear you great! I’ve got numbers [breaks up]
Uh, this is Skippy. What’s goin’ on?
Hey, I’m gonna bring my straw in there—my flexible straw, okay?—
And I’m gonna dip it into some different bottles, some different cans, and just try out some different liquors tonight, okay? Just lemme figure out what I wanna buy, alright?

It means I’m gonna bring my straw in there and I’m gonna dip it in things, crack a few bottles open, try some different things out, try some different flavors! M’kay? [delay] How’s that jibe with ya there, guy? How late y’all open, anyway?
Got it! Thank you, thank you!
Thank you for calling Waipahu O’Reilly’s. How—how can I help you?
Yeah, hey, I need to get like a new VIN number, like, right away, like, pronto. Can you help me with that ’n hook me up? [exhales]
How can I hook you up with a VIN number?
It’s not important right now. I just need to get a brand new VIN number immediately! Alright, I can pay big money, alright? [inhales]
So how late are you guys there, then? [exhales]—
How late are you guys there tonight? Also, how do you—how do you shut off a car alarm, like remotely? I need to make it stop like right away.
Unhook the battery? Okay, now I—how much for a new VIN number, then?—
Uh, we don’t sell VIN numbers.
I can give ya four, five dollars.
Yeah, we—we don’t sell VIN numbers. The car comes with the VIN.
Okay, hey, how do I get like a huge, big dent out of a white car? And like stains and stuff? I need to get that right off right away, uh, here tonight.
Okay. Why don’t you work on that VIN number for me, alright?—
—You have reached the maximum time permitted for recording your message—
I’ll be in there in a minute, okay? Alright, I’m with ya now, buddy—
Yeah! How do—how do I get a new VIN number for my car? I need to get one right away.
You can’t. You have to do that through the DMV. We don’t [unclear]—
—I can’t—I need to go a different direction, buddy.
Okay so, we don’t do anything with VIN numbers.
Okay, uh, can you help me shut off a car alarm, like remotely? I need to make it stop.
You can take out the battery.
Yeah, it’s in someone else’s car, though. Should I just go in and unhook the battery or…?
You’re—you’re callin’ the wrong people, man.
Well, I need a VIN number, guy! It’s—it’s urgent. It’s imperative—
So, call the—call someone else, alright?
I don’t have time for that. You’re in my neighborhood and, uh, it’s kinda your—
—Alright, yeah, we don’t do anything involving VIN numbers.
Guy, it’s your duty to help me out, alright? So just do what you can!—
Thank you, Major Entertainer! Denver, Colorado woo! [delay] Oriental Theater! Oriental, Oriental, Oriental! Asian Theater, okay. Major Entertainer, do you have some nu—?
Do you have some numerals for me? Please.
I’ve got a lot of—so here’s a—here’s one right now: RJ Cooper.
[samples of Wayne Bent ] We are naked and unashamed— Uh, MDs are with naked people— MDs even put their fingers in w—
Yeah, I got your number from, ah, Old Ironsides up at the filling station, there. Guy’s a friend of mine up ‘ere! [exhales]
Oh! Nice! Yeah, ya know, just kinda doin’ the do, if you—if you will. If he did, anyway—
—Doin’ ya thing, doin’ ya thing? He doin’ he thing, she doin’ she thing, uh?

—Look, man, I’ve noticed you were outside a lot the last couple days. I’m kinda new to the area and that’s something I observed. But tomorrow morning, uh, would you mind stayin’ indoors? I got a 28-foot horsewhip I like to practice with. I just wanna make sure everybody’s outta the way—inside, not outside, ya know what I’m sayin’? I don’t need the, ah, need the liability, the responsibility.
Well I’m sure I could gallivant a little bit. And, uh, try and keep my, you know—
—If that’d be agreeable with you, sir.
Oh, yeah. I mean, I get this p—this spinnin’ around, it’s like a propeller flyin’ through the air! [exhales] [delay, pitch-up] So let’s get together on that and, ah, whip some stuff loose—
—here, in the mornin’—in the AM—bright ’n early! How’s that sound to ya?
Uh, that sounds great. Unfortunately, I’ve got a big meetin’ tomorrow so…

—Um, I’d be willing—well, yeah. I mean, I’d be willing to meet up, uh, if that’d be agreeable with you at some specific juncture tomorrow morning. But, ya know [inhales] there’s just too much noise in the neighborhood, ya know?
I’m afraid my whip and my goings-on take precedent, so… [inhales]
Yay, how ya doin? XYZ Place?
Doin’ well, yes! You’ve reached us!
Hey, do you have a jukebox up ‘ere?
Yeah, it’s the—it’s a TouchTunes jukebox type-deal. It’s got like every stupid song you could think of.
You got, ah, Skip to My Lou by Sally Jessy Rafael up ‘ere?
Uh, I have no idea, man! There are literally millions of songs on that thing. It’s all digital and—
—So I’m sure if you searched it, I—I would bet it would come up.
Is anybody there? We’re getting disconnected. I’m terribly sorry. That’s kinda par for the course here in LPC land. I’m very sorry. Hopefully you can hear me here tonight.
Tip with a T, as in “tortoise”. Ya feel me?—
Tip, yeah. What’re ya doin’ tonight? [exhales]
Uh, uh, not much. I’m just watchin’ TV. I got home from work at like 7. I’m just chillin’—

—Yeah. Yeah? Hey, um, I’m gonna be goin’ out of town and I, uh, I already talked to the post office. I’m just gonna have my stuff sent up there—my mail—for about three weeks to your—to your place, alright? So, just givin’ ya a heads-up on that. Starting, uh—
—You’re having your stuff sent to my place?—
—starting this weekend. Yeah, over to your place, uh-huh! Just wanted to make sure that you had a heads-up on that. Is that okay?
Y—yes. So, just didn’t want you to be alarmed when you see my stuff, uh, arrive at your place. I’m kind of new in the area; I don’t know anybody here, you know?
And, uh, you know, if you see anything real urgent or anything like that that’s, you know, really pressing, just—if you’d go ahead and pay it, I’ll absolutely reimburse you, okay?
No, Tip. “T-I-P”. Tippy. You never heard of me?
At—at the beginning of the conversation, I asked you if it was “Kip” and you said, “No, it’s Kit with a T”.
No, I didn’t. I said it’s Tip with a T. And, uh—
You know, like, I ha—I got a leak in my, uh, roof and I—I had to use a lotta water. Things like that are gonna result in some bills. Please go ‘head and pay them, sort the rest of my mail—
I’m—first of all, I don’t know anyone named Kip or Kit. And you’re asking me to pay bills and you’re shipping your shit to my house?
Well, just be a good sport, ya know, I’m new to the area up here!—
—Who do you—? How—? How did you—? How do you know me?
Well, I got your number from Old Ironsides up at the filling station; guy’s a friend o’ mine up ‘ere! [exhales]
I just don’t want ya to be shocked when my mail shows up there, okay?
[beep] Your recording has reached the maximum length—
To delete and re-record your message, press “3”.

Okay. Later. Okay. [sharp inhale] Denver, Colorado! [reverb, delay, pitch-down] Thank you for being here! It’s outstanding! Oriental Theater, thank you for having us tonight. Thank you to Major Entertainer and to Jimmy and to Evan and to everybody down there! I sure do appreciate ya time!
Yeah, this is Tip. What’re ya doin’, man?
Tip, “T-I-P”. Can ya hear or what?
Hey, guy, you ready to feel my frickin’ grip?! [exhales] Once I show up up ‘ere?
You ready to feel my freakin’ grip, guy, once I grip ‘hold of your neck, or…?
I’m Tippy. I’m new to the area up here and I don’t like what I see, so, uh, you and I are gonna get together and work things out! [exhales] Ya feel me? [exhales] I’ll meet you tonight, guy—
— Get a slice of pizza and then rough ya up a little bit, flatten ya out! [exhales]
I’m new. My name’s Tip and I’m ready to grip you by the lip. [exhales] [reverb] Does that work for you or what? [exhales] [delay, pitch-down]
Does. It. Work for you, Charlie? Speak up. Okay, no? You outta here? Good! Good for you. [inhales] Good for you, good for you, good for you. Okay, we’re movin’ right along, yes we are! Um, okay.
Oh, shit! [laughs] Oh, fuck! [laughs] [sharp inhale] [groans] Oh, my god! [exhales]
Hi! I am so sorry we got chopped off, there. Oriental Theater—
—I don’t know. He left the stage.
